title: 甦れ！　ゴルシ印のソース焼きそば！

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
As I make my way to the exit after watching a
race, I notice an Umamusume holding her head in
despair.

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
Uwaaah... Business has been so bad lately. At this
rate, the future of my Golshi-brand yakisoba will
go up in delicious smoke...

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
Hey, you there! Gimme an idea on how to pull my
business out of its freewheeling nosedive!
choice_data_list:
Why don't you try improving the flavor of your
yakisoba?

Why don't you try selling grilled akashiyaki
dumplings?

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
If I make it any tastier than it already is, it'll
jeopardize all the other yakisoba joints in the
galaxy!

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
Mediocre quality brings more peace of mind...
Which means!

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
The flavor is fine just as it is! Yeaaah, buddy! I
have no idea why, but I'm pumped!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: I guess what's important is motivation.

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
Get outta here! Of all the things you could've
suggested, akashiyaki?! Everyone knows takoyaki
would've been the better suggestion!

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
Besides, the galactic economy would go haywire
if I sell anything other than yakisoba!

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
As a token of my gratitude, I'll tell ya the most
important thing when it comes to making
takoyaki.

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text:
You listening? It's all about rhythm. Start off nice
and gentle, then crank up the speed at the end!

text_block_list.name: Gold Ship
text_block_list.text: Ya gotta feel the beat too, yo!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Start off gentle then gradually speed up... That
might actually be a good tip when running on
certain tracks...
